description.text: Definition of the Submodel CapabilityDescription identified by its semanticId. The Submodel idShort can be picked freely.
A Set of CapabilityContainer for a Use Case for the asset.
A Container for one capability and all its additional descriptive elements.
[A capability is a] implementation-independent specification of a function in industrial production to achieve an effect in the physical or virtual world.
Individual comment of the capability.
Set of properties describing the capability in more detail, if existing.
Information for a certain property as defined by CapabilityPropertyType and its descriptive elements.
Relationship of the Property described in the Property container as first element and the identical property as second element in another Submodel or an external information source.
Range made of min and max values forming an interval. A valueId shall be set to define the semantic for the values.
Property with a value describing an information data point. A valueId shall be set to define the semantic for the value.
Property with a value for one or more language entries with corresponding text describing an information data point. A valueId shall be set to define the semantic for the value.
A list of one or more elements defined by only the enum type CapabilityPropertyType.
Range made of min and max values forming an interval. A valueId shall be set to define the semantic for the values.
Property with a value describing an information data point. A valueId shall be set to define the semantic for the value.
Property with a value for one or more language entries with corresponding text describing an information data point. A valueId shall be set to define the semantic for the value.
A list of one or more elements defined by only the enum type CapabilityPropertyType.
General description of the property.
Collection of relationships for the capability, if existing.
Relationship between the Capability element in the CapabilityContainer as first element and a Skill implementation, not defined in this Submodel template, as second element.
If composition(s) for the Capability element in the CapabilityContainer exists, this set has to be created.
Container corresponding to one composition for the Capability in the CapabilityContainer.
Relationship between a composed capability as first element and one of its minimum two subordinate capabilities as second element.
Comment to describe the composition in human readable form.
If generalization(s) for the Capability element in the CapabilityContainer exists, this set has to be created.
Relationship between the Capability as first element, described in the CapabilityContainer, and a more general Capability as second element.
If constraint(s) for the Capability element in the CapabilityContainer exists, this set has to be created.
If one or more constraints exist for a Capability Property, then for every constraint a PropertyConstraintContainer has to be created.
Property element which can be used to validate the constraint for the considered Properties in this PropertyConstraintContainer against other properties.

SubmodelElement which can be used to validate the constraint for the considered Properties in this PropertyConstraintContainer against other properties. This can be freely defined for the purpose of constraining a property and is not specified in this Submodel Template.
Object Contraint Language (OCL) as File element which can be used to validate the constraint for the considered Properties in this PropertyConstraintContainer against other properties.
Reference to an (external) Operation element which can be used to validate the constraint for the considered Properties in this PropertyConstraintContainer against other properties.

Abstract Enum type of allowed SubmodelElements for these Properties constraints. Exactly one of the SubmodelElements below must be instanciated, e.g., similar to SubmodelElementList with exactly one element.
Defines the type of the property conditions as defined in the ConceptDescription with the same name (PropertyConditionalType).
Contains all relationships for the constraint in the PropertyConstraintContainer.
Relates the PropertyConstraint as first element to a Property from a PropertyContainer as second element.
If one or more constraints exist for a Capability, then for every transitional constraint a TransitionConstraintContainer has to be created.
Relates the constrained Capability as first element to a constraining Capability from another CapabilityContainer as second element.
Defines the element TransitionConstrainedBy of TransitionConstraintType.

definition.text: Enumeration of transition conditional types for capability constraints: Pre, Post, Invariant, and combinations.
Enumeration of property conditional types for capability constraints: Pre, Post, Invariant, and combinations.
